operating_state: Total revenue increased 24% to $23.33 billion driven by automotive sales growth from 108,378 additional Model 3/Y deliveries despite lower average selling prices from year-over-year price reductions. Energy generation and storage revenue surged 148% from increased Megapack deployments and higher solar cash deployments at elevated prices. Services revenue grew 44% from increased used vehicle sales volume and expanded maintenance services as the fleet grows.
cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed from 29.1% to 19.3% as automotive gross margin declined from 32.9% to 21.1% due to higher raw material, manufacturing, logistics and warranty costs partially offset by IRA manufacturing credits. Energy storage gross margin improved from negative 11.7% to positive 11.0% driven by higher-margin sales mix and operational improvements.
material_events: Company entered a $5.0 billion senior unsecured revolving credit facility with potential expansion to $7.0 billion, terminating the prior ABL credit agreement. Board adopted bylaw amendments providing stockholders proxy access rights for director nominations and updating advance notice procedures. Capital expenditures reached $2.1 billion primarily supporting manufacturing expansion.
macro_exposures: Inflationary pressures increased across operating markets while central banks raised interest rates rapidly, impacting vehicle lease and finance arrangement affordability. Foreign currency translation adjustments provided $130 million positive impact compared to prior year negative impact. Supply chain dependencies on single-source suppliers remain a concentration risk amid global trade condition changes and labor availability constraints.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures between $7.0 to $9.0 billion annually through 2025 to support global project pipeline including factory expansions and new product development. Company remains focused on increasing vehicle production capacity, reducing costs, improving FSD capabilities, and expanding global infrastructure while ramping Cybertruck tooling and Tesla Semi pilot production. Energy storage production ramp continues with announced new Megafactory in Shanghai.
uncertainty_profile: DOJ investigation into Autopilot and FSD features creates potential for material adverse impact if enforcement action pursued. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings include SolarCity acquisition litigation with Delaware Supreme Court appeal pending and CEO performance award derivative case awaiting post-trial decision. Manufacturing ramp uncertainties persist from new product and manufacturing technologies, concurrent international projects, and industry-wide component constraints that may impact ambitious technological targets.
